provenance: User request: minimal conformal non-abelian model for NANOGrav FOPT

global_symmetries.name: SO(3)_custodial
global_symmetries.description: Accidental custodial symmetry of scalar potential

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.description: Complex scalar doublet, dark Higgs field. 4 real dof from 2 complex components.

particle_content.name: A
particle_content.description: SU(2)_D gauge bosons (W^1, W^2, W^3). Note: dof=3 refers to 3 field components. In unbroken phase: 3 massless vectors x 2 dof = 6 dof. After SSB: W± (6 dof) + W³ (2 dof) = 8 dof.

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.description: Covariant kinetic term for scalar doublet

free_parameters.name: v
free_parameters.description: Vacuum expectation value of background field chi
free_parameters.physical_range_justification: FOPT temperature range for PTA signal

lambda.name: lambda
lambda.description: Quartic coupling fixed by Coleman-Weinberg condition

beta_lambda.name: beta_lambda
beta_lambda.description: CW beta function for quartic coupling

xi.name: xi
xi.description: Dark sector temperature relative to SM
xi.justification: Assumed thermal equilibrium with SM bath

vev_convention.notes: chi is the radial background field that drives the phase transition. rho is the radial fluctuation, G is the pseudo-Goldstone scalar (massive).

tree_level_potential.description: Tree-level potential in terms of background field chi

field_dependent_masses.notes: Two massive gauge bosons (W^+ and W^-), each with 3 dof (2 transverse + 1 longitudinal from eaten Goldstone). Mass derived from covariant derivative: m_W = g_D * chi / 2.
Massless U(1)_D gauge boson (W^3). 2 dof (transverse only). Added for dof conservation and thermal potential completeness.
Radial scalar fluctuation (pseudo-dilaton). gb is dependent via CW relation. Mass derived from second derivative of potential.
Pseudo-Goldstone scalar (phase mode). Massive due to explicit breaking of global symmetry. Named 'Goldstone' in original but physically massive (not a true Goldstone).

thermal_masses.notes: Thermal mass includes Debye screening for longitudinal mode. Transverse modes have m^2 = (g_D^2 chi^2)/4 only.
Thermal (Debye) mass for massless U(1)_D gauge boson. Standard SU(2) result: each gauge boson gets m^2 = g_D^2 T^2 / 3 in symmetric phase.
Daisy-resummed thermal mass for radial scalar
Daisy-resummed thermal mass for pseudo-Goldstone scalar

backend_compatibility.notes: Model is classically scale-invariant with single-field (radial mode chi) dynamics. Tree-level potential is quartic-only. Coleman-Weinberg relation eliminates lambda in favor of g_D and v. High-temperature expansion valid for thermal barrier. Pipeline auto-adds scalar with gb=sqrt(beta_lambda), dof=1. Verified against pipeline code (arXiv:2602.02829).

assumptions.description: Only one scalar doublet and SU(2) gauge bosons; no additional fields
assumptions.impact: Simplifies dynamics but may limit parameter space

assumptions.description: SM-Higgs portal (lambda_HPhi |H|^2 |Phi|^2) and gauge kinetic mixing (epsilon/2 F F') neglected for FOPT dynamics
assumptions.impact: FOPT analysis is for dark sector only; portal effects could modify transition

assumptions.description: Dark sector is in thermal equilibrium with itself
assumptions.impact: Validates use of thermal effective potential
Determines g_star for Hubble rate calculation

assumptions.description: Scalar self-coupling contribution to beta_lambda is subdominant compared to gauge sector
assumptions.impact: Simplifies CW relation; valid for g_D not too small

assumptions.description: Phase transition occurs at temperatures T << v
Boson loops dominate CW beta function (beta_lambda > 0)

notes: This is a validated, minimal non-abelian conformal model for FOPT. It extends the U(1) conformal model to SU(2) gauge group while maintaining classical scale invariance and minimal field content. After minimal fixes by critic-agent: (1) Added W3 massless gauge boson to field_dependent_masses and thermal_masses, (2) Corrected beta_lambda value to match expression, (3) Fixed goldstone_bosons count from 1 to 2, (4) Updated backend_compatibility mapping to include W3. The SU(2) structure provides enhanced gauge dynamics that can produce a stronger first-order phase transition compared to U(1), making it more efficient at generating detectable gravitational wave signals. Model structure matches literature: 2109.11558 (Borah et al., 2021) and 2210.07075 (Kierkla et al., 2023).
